concerns.text: SC-001 requires validation of visual complexity metrics against 'human-rated complexity scores from a pilot study (n=20)'. The plan (Summary, Technical Context, Research Strategy) explicitly states 'lack of real human participants' and relies entirely on 'simulated experimental data generation'. There is no plan element (phase, task, or data model) to acquire human ratings or conduct the pilot study required by SC-001. This is an unbacked success criterion.

SC-003 requires measuring reaction-time difference against a 'baseline reaction time... for the same participant'. The plan's data model (Participant Measurement) and simulation strategy generate synthetic data based on 'stimulus complexity + random noise' but do not define a mechanism to establish or measure a 'baseline' condition per participant as required by the spec. The plan lacks a task to simulate or record this specific baseline comparison.

SC-004 requires measuring the 'family-wise error rate' against the nominal alpha level. The plan mentions applying 'Benjamini-Hochberg procedure' (FR-004) but does not include a specific task, phase, or output metric to calculate and report the *actual* family-wise error rate (FWER) or Type I error rate from the simulation to verify it against the nominal alpha. The plan addresses the correction method but not the measurement of the error rate itself.
